cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the actions of a search group formed by American scientists in relation to the Loch Ness monster. First, I need to recall the details from the passage. The key part is the paragraph from 1975. It says: "In 1975, however, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera. It took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature. Its body was about four meters long and had a very ugly head on the end of a four meter neck. Many people then began to believe in the monster. But even today we can not be certain." So the American scientists used an underwater camera that took pictures every 70 seconds. Some of those pictures seemed to show a creature that resembled the monster. So the key point here is that they took pictures which "seemed to show a monster," not that they definitely found it. Now let's look at the options: (A) found the monster itself - The passage doesn't say they found the actual monster, just that some pictures seemed to show it. So this is probably incorrect. (B) found a huge cave under water - The passage mentions that underwater television cameras found a huge cave, but that was in the part about 1961 and after, not the 1975 American scientists. So this is not the correct answer for the question about the American group. (C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all - The passage doesn't say that the scientists believed there wasn't a monster. It says that after their findings, many people began to believe in the monster. So this is also incorrect. (D) took some pictures which seemed to show a monster - This matches the
